title: The road is only going to get harder for Bruins after ’embarrassing’ loss to Leafs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins lost 4-2 to the struggling Toronto Maple Leafs, missing a key opportunity to secure playoff points.
Coach Marco Sturm criticized the team's lack of effort despite goalie Jeremy Swayman's strong performance with 31 saves.
Boston holds the first wild-card spot in the Eastern Conference but faces tough upcoming games against the Sabres and Wild.

title: Adam Schefter: No ‘under-the-table’ A.J. Brown trade in place for Patriots, Eagles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Adam Schefter denied rumors of an 'under-the-table' deal between Patriots and Eagles for A.J. Brown.
Trading A.J. Brown before June 1 would cost Eagles $43.5 million in dead money, after would be $16.353 million.
Patriots remain interested in A.J. Brown but may pursue other wide receivers before any potential deal post-June 1.

title: This police officer is running Boston to support self-defense training
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Caroline Butts, a police officer from Milton, is running her third Boston Marathon to support The Vanessa Marcotte Foundation.
The foundation focuses on violence prevention, self-defense, and female empowerment to help women live fearlessly and safely.
Butts aims to raise awareness against violence towards women by combining her role in law enforcement with her passion for running.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 25)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington property at 333 Groveland St. sold for $875,000, built in 1900 with 9 bedrooms and 3 baths.
Acton property at 2 Huron Road, 4 bedrooms, 5 baths, sold for $1,325,000 on a 24,000-square-foot lot.
Boston condo at 397-399 Commonwealth Ave. #1 sold for $6,725,000, built in 1900 with 3 bedrooms and 3 baths.

title: Man convicted more than 3 decades after raping 2 women at gunpoint in Framingham store
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, was convicted for the 1989 rape of two women in a Framingham store.
Gale was identified through DNA and captured in 2024 after evading police for over three decades.
Gale will be sentenced on March 30 after being arrested following a car chase in Los Angeles.

title: New Providence basketball coach goes viral for recruiting joke: ‘Just look at my wife’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bryan Hodgson introduced as Providence basketball head coach after success at South Florida and Arkansas State.
Hodgson made a viral recruiting joke about his wife Jordan at his inaugural press conference.
The Friars have faced two consecutive losing seasons and Hodgson aims to revitalize the team.

title: He’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alex Castrichini from Concord, Mass., is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support children in foster care.
Castrichini is motivated by his involvement with Boston CASA, which advocates for foster children navigating complex systems.
The runner is inspired by stories from the Boston CASA Gala and serves on the organization's board.

title: ‘Children’s lives are at stake’: Boston teachers protest staffing cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston teachers, parents, and students protested at City Hall against proposed cuts of over 400 Boston Public School jobs.
The proposed budget cuts would affect educators and staff working with high-needs students, requiring a $48 million investment to prevent layoffs.
Boston Student Advisory Council urged investment in education, warning cuts would harm multilingual learners and students with IEPs.

title: Where ‘No Kings’ protests are happening around Massachusetts this Saturday
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts will host over 160 'No Kings' protests on March 28, one of the largest mobilizations in the U.S.
Protests address concerns over immigration policies and democracy, fueled by U.S. involvement in Iran and Trump's actions.
RSVP is required for some events to receive location details; major events include rallies in Boston and Cambridge.

title: ESPN’s ‘Pardon The Interruption’ hosts believe Jaylen Brown should win NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN's Wilbon and Kornheiser endorse Jaylen Brown as the NBA MVP frontrunner on 'Pardon The Interruption'.
Jaylen Brown averages career highs with 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.1 assists over 64 games this season.
Boston Celtics hold second place in the Eastern Conference despite Jayson Tatum's prolonged absence due to injury.
